componentDescription: CodeEditor provides a full-featured code editing area with syntax highlighting, line numbers, and editor features like auto-indent. Use it for in-app code editing scenarios — configuration editors, query builders, script editors, and developer-facing settings.

usageExamples.title: JSON configuration editor
usageExamples.description: Editable JSON config with syntax highlighting.

usageExamples.title: Read-only SQL query display
usageExamples.description: Non-editable display of a generated SQL query.

usageExamples.title: JavaScript script editor
usageExamples.description: Full editor for user-defined JavaScript automation scripts.

value.llmContext: Required. Controlled code string. Manage with useState. Initialize with default code or empty string. For large initial values loaded from an API, show a Skeleton until loaded.

onChange.llmContext: Required. Called with the updated code string on every edit. This fires on every keystroke — debounce any API calls or parsing operations in your handler.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with updated code string on edit

language.llmContext: Syntax highlighting language. Common values: 'javascript', 'typescript', 'json', 'python', 'sql', 'yaml', 'html', 'css', 'markdown'. Match to the actual content type for correct highlighting.

readOnly.llmContext: Set to true to display code without allowing edits. Use for showing generated code, API responses, or example snippets. For read-only code display with a copy button, consider CodeBlock instead.

theme.llmContext: 'light' (default) for standard UIs. Use 'dark' for dedicated developer tool contexts or when the surrounding UI is dark-themed.

height.llmContext: Fixed pixel height of the editor area. Set based on expected content length. For short configs, 200-300px. For scripts, 400-600px. For full-page editors, use a CSS height instead.
height.propDescription: Height of the editor area in pixels
